Title: Time-Travel My Selfie Into Any Era (Same Face)
Teaser: Drops your exact face into a different decade — hair, wardrobe, film stock and styling all shift to the period, but the person staring back is unmistakably you.
PromptHint: Attach your selfie, name the [ERA] (e.g. 1970s, 1920s, Y2K 2000s), and optionally a [SETTING] and [MOOD].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a period-accurate portrait stylist and retoucher working inside a conversational image editor (GPT-4o / Gemini). TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and restyle it as if the same person were photographed in the [ERA] — keep their identity, but change everything around it to fit the decade. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): list what actually signals that era — the hairstyle, the eyewear, the fabric and cut of clothing, the color grading and grain of the era's cameras, the background props — then apply only those. THEN EDIT: restyle the hair and wardrobe to a believable [ERA] look, place them in a [SETTING] that fits the period, and grade the whole frame to match that era's photography (film stock, color cast, grain, lens character). Carry a [MOOD] through the lighting and expression. CONSTRAINTS: keep the same face geometry, skin tone, eye color and core features unmistakably recognizable — do NOT beautify, slim, or morph them into a different person; one person only; period-accurate details (no anachronistic logos, phones, or modern clothing); natural five-finger hands; no text or watermarks. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished era-restyled portrait only, then on a new line give me one short follow-up sentence I can paste back to push the era earlier/later or swap the outfit.

Title: Cinematic Movie-Still Glow-Up Of My Selfie
Teaser: Turns a plain phone selfie into a frame that looks pulled from a feature film — anamorphic lighting, color grade and shallow depth of field — while your face stays exactly as it is.
PromptHint: Attach your selfie, set the [FILM GENRE/LOOK] (e.g. neo-noir thriller, A24 drama, sci-fi blockbuster) and optionally the [LOCATION].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a cinematographer and DI colorist re-lighting a portrait inside a conversational image editor. TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and re-grade it into a single cinematic movie still in the style of a [FILM GENRE/LOOK], without changing who the person is. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the key-light direction, the practical light sources, the color palette of that genre, and the lens character (focal length, depth of field, any anamorphic flare) before touching the image. THEN EDIT: relight the face with motivated, directional cinema lighting and soft fill; place them in a fitting [LOCATION] rendered with a shallow, filmic depth of field; apply a genre-true color grade with controlled contrast, subtle film grain, and gentle bloom in the highlights. Keep skin texture real — pores and all — not airbrushed. CONSTRAINTS: keep the exact same face, expression, hairstyle and identity recognizable — no slimming, no smoothing into a stranger; one person; photoreal, no plastic skin; no on-screen text, subtitles, or logos; natural hands. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished cinematic still only, then on a new line one short sentence I can paste back to change the genre or move the key light.

Title: Restyle My Selfie Into Any Outfit + Location
Teaser: A wardrobe-and-scene swap that keeps your face and pose but dresses you in a new [OUTFIT] and teleports you to a new [LOCATION] — like a virtual photoshoot.
PromptHint: Attach your selfie, describe the [OUTFIT] (main change), the [LOCATION], and an optional [TIME OF DAY].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a fashion-editorial photographer and digital stylist working inside a conversational image editor. TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and restyle the same person into a new [OUTFIT] photographed on location at [LOCATION]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how the outfit drapes on their actual body, what footwear and accessories complete it, and how the location's ambient light should fall on them so the relight looks real. THEN EDIT: dress them in the described [OUTFIT] with believable fabric folds, fit and texture; place them naturally in [LOCATION] with matching perspective and scale; light the scene for [TIME OF DAY] so shadows, color temperature and reflections agree with the environment. Keep their pose and gaze coherent with the new framing. CONSTRAINTS: keep the same face, skin tone, hair and body proportions unmistakably them — do NOT idealize or reshape their body; realistic fabric and lighting integration (no pasted-on cutout look); one person; natural five-finger hands; no logos, brand marks, or text. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished restyled photo only, then on a new line one short sentence I can paste back to change the outfit color or swap the backdrop.

Title: Fantasy Avatar Of Me — Elf, Mage Or Warrior
Teaser: Casts your real face as a fantasy hero — pointed-ear elf, robed mage, or armored warrior — with painterly lighting, while your features stay clearly yours under the makeup and gear.
PromptHint: Attach your selfie, pick the [ARCHETYPE] (e.g. high elf ranger, storm mage, frost knight) and an optional [SETTING] + [PALETTE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a concept artist for a high-end fantasy game, working inside a conversational image editor. TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and reimagine this exact person as a [ARCHETYPE] fantasy character, keeping their face recognizable beneath the costume and styling. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide which fantasy cues sell the archetype — costume materials (cloth, leather, metal), props (staff, blade, glow), subtle face styling (war paint, faint freckles, ear shape) — and a believable light source like firelight, moonlight, or arcane glow. THEN EDIT: dress them in archetype-appropriate gear with rich material detail, place them in a [SETTING] with atmospheric depth, and render the whole frame in a painterly, cinematic palette tuned to [PALETTE]. Let the magic light interact with their skin and hair. CONSTRAINTS: keep the same underlying face structure, eye color and expression so it's clearly THEM in costume — do not replace them with a generic model; one character only; cohesive fantasy world (no modern objects, no text or logos); anatomically correct hands; tasteful, family-friendly. Optional Midjourney render: append --ar 4:5 --style raw --stylize 250. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished fantasy portrait only, then on a new line one short sentence I can paste back to change the archetype or the magic-light color.

Title: Golden-Hour Professional Headshot From A Casual Selfie
Teaser: Upgrades a quick mirror selfie into a warm, LinkedIn-ready headshot — flattering golden-hour light, clean background, and crisp wardrobe — without faking a new face.
PromptHint: Attach your selfie, choose the [BACKGROUND] (e.g. soft office bokeh, neutral studio gray) and an optional [ATTIRE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a professional headshot photographer and gentle retoucher inside a conversational image editor. TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and turn it into a polished, professional headshot of the same person. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the flattering light pattern (soft loop or Rembrandt), the catchlights in the eyes, and a clean background that won't compete with the face. THEN EDIT: relight with warm, soft golden-hour-style key light and gentle fill; tidy the wardrobe into [ATTIRE] if specified; replace the busy background with [BACKGROUND] at a shallow depth of field; do light, honest retouching — even skin tone and reduced shine, but keep real pores, lines and texture. Add bright, natural catchlights. CONSTRAINTS: keep the exact same face, age, skin tone, hairstyle and identity — NO slimming, NO airbrushing into a younger or different person; eyes sharp and natural; one person; photoreal; no text, no logos, no over-saturation. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished headshot only, then on a new line one short sentence I can paste back to change the background or warm/cool the light.

Title: Drop My Selfie Into A Famous City At Night
Teaser: Teleports you onto a neon-lit street in a city of your choice — wet pavement reflections, signage glow, atmospheric haze — with your face and lighting blended in so it reads as a real travel shot.
PromptHint: Attach your selfie, name the [CITY/STREET] (e.g. Shibuya crossing, Times Square) and an optional [WEATHER] + [VIBE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a travel-portrait photographer compositing on location inside a conversational image editor. TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and place the same person into a nighttime street scene in [CITY/STREET], lit by the city itself. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide where the dominant light comes from (signage, shopfronts, headlights), how it should color the face, and how reflections and haze build depth behind them. THEN EDIT: surround them with the chosen city's recognizable nighttime character — neon and signage glow, bokeh crowd lights, wet-pavement reflections, gentle [WEATHER] atmosphere; relight their face and clothing with colored ambient spill so they belong in the scene; carry a [VIBE] through grade and contrast. Match camera height and perspective to a natural handheld shot. CONSTRAINTS: keep the same face, hair, outfit and identity recognizable — no beautifying into a stranger; lighting on the subject must agree with the scene (no flat pasted look); one person; no readable real brand logos or invented gibberish text on signs; natural hands. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished street portrait only, then on a new line one short sentence I can paste back to change the city or the weather.

Title: Renaissance Oil-Painting Portrait Of Me
Teaser: Repaints your selfie as a museum oil portrait — chiaroscuro light, visible brushwork, period dress — keeping your likeness as the unmistakable sitter.
PromptHint: Attach your selfie, pick the [PAINTING STYLE] (e.g. Rembrandt chiaroscuro, Vermeer soft light) and an optional [COSTUME].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an old-master portrait painter recreating a sitter inside a conversational image editor. TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and repaint the same person as a classical oil portrait in the spirit of [PAINTING STYLE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the single dramatic light source, the dark tonal background, the period [COSTUME], and where visible brushstrokes should catch the light. THEN EDIT: render them with painterly skin, soft chiaroscuro modeling, a deep shadowed background, and rich impasto highlights on fabric and jewelry; dress them in believable period attire; keep brush texture visible rather than photographic smoothness. CONSTRAINTS: keep the same face structure, expression and likeness so the sitter is clearly THEM — not a generic painted face; one sitter; cohesive period look (no modern items, no text or signatures unless asked); painterly, not plastic; correct hands if visible. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished oil portrait only, then on a new line one short sentence I can paste back to change the painter's style or the costume.

Title: Cyberpunk Future Version Of My Selfie
Teaser: Reimagines you in a neon cyberpunk future — subtle tech wardrobe, holographic city glow, rain-slick reflections — with your real face front and center.
PromptHint: Attach your selfie, set the [TECH LEVEL] (e.g. street-samurai, corpo-exec, netrunner) and an optional [NEON PALETTE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a sci-fi key-art artist working inside a conversational image editor. TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and restyle the same person as a [TECH LEVEL] character in a neon cyberpunk future. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the wardrobe (techwear layers, subtle augments, reflective fabrics), the dominant neon light direction, and how holographic signage and rain should color the face. THEN EDIT: dress them in believable near-future streetwear with restrained tech accents, place them on a rain-lit megacity street with holographic glow, and grade the frame in a [NEON PALETTE] with cyan/magenta rim light, atmospheric haze and wet reflections. Keep augments subtle so the face still leads. CONSTRAINTS: keep the same face, eyes, hairstyle and identity clearly recognizable — no full robot/mask replacement, no morphing into a different person; one person; cohesive future world (no real brand logos, no gibberish text on signs); natural hands. Optional Midjourney render: append --ar 4:5 --style raw --stylize 300. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished cyberpunk portrait only, then on a new line one short sentence I can paste back to dial the augments up/down or change the neon colors.

Title: Seasonal Restyle — Same Selfie, Four Moods

PromptHint: Attach your selfie, pick the [SEASON] (autumn / winter / spring / summer) and an optional [SCENE] + [TIME OF DAY].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a lifestyle photographer and stylist re-dressing a portrait for the season inside a conversational image editor. TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and restyle the same person for [SEASON], changing wardrobe, background and light to match. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the season's signature wardrobe layer, the ambient color temperature, the environmental cues (falling leaves, snow, blossoms, warm haze), and how that light should fall on the face. THEN EDIT: dress them in season-appropriate clothing, place them in a fitting [SCENE], grade the frame to the season's palette, and light it for [TIME OF DAY] with believable shadows and atmosphere (e.g. breath fog in cold, warm rim light in summer). CONSTRAINTS: keep the same face, hairstyle, skin tone and identity recognizable — no beautifying or reshaping; realistic fabric and weather integration; one person; natural hands; no text or logos. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished seasonal portrait only, then on a new line one short sentence I can paste back to switch the season or the time of day.

Title: Black-And-White Editorial Portrait From My Selfie
Teaser: Converts your selfie into a high-end monochrome editorial portrait — deep blacks, sculpted light, fine grain — the kind that runs full-page in a magazine, with your face intact.
PromptHint: Attach your selfie, choose the [LIGHTING STYLE] (e.g. dramatic single-source, soft beauty light) and an optional [BACKDROP].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a black-and-white editorial portrait photographer and printer working inside a conversational image editor. TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and reinterpret it as a refined monochrome editorial portrait of the same person. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the tonal contrast curve, the [LIGHTING STYLE] and its shadow shape, the eye catchlights, and the grain/texture that gives it a printed-film feel. THEN EDIT: relight the face per [LIGHTING STYLE] with sculpted highlights and rich, controlled shadows; place them against [BACKDROP]; convert to a tonally deep black-and-white with full blacks, clean whites, fine film grain and real skin texture. Keep the gaze and expression compelling. CONSTRAINTS: keep the exact same face, age and identity — no smoothing into a different or younger person; preserve pores and natural lines; one person; no color, no text, no logos; natural hands if visible. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished monochrome portrait only, then on a new line one short sentence I can paste back to change the contrast or the lighting style.

Title: Put My Selfie On A Magazine Cover
Teaser: Stages your selfie as the cover star of a glossy magazine — masthead, cover lines and pro lighting — with your real face as the hero, and clean spell-checked text.
PromptHint: Attach your selfie, set the [MAGAZINE NAME], the [THEME/GENRE] (e.g. fashion, tech, travel) and an optional [COVER LINE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a magazine cover art director and retoucher working inside a conversational image editor. TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and design a believable glossy magazine cover with this exact person as the cover star. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the cover crop and pose, where the masthead sits behind/over the hair, the typographic hierarchy, and a relight that gives the face cover-quality polish. THEN EDIT: relight and lightly retouch the subject for an editorial finish (keep real texture); compose a clean cover layout with the masthead '[MAGAZINE NAME]' at the top in a style fitting [THEME/GENRE], a short bold [COVER LINE], and 2-3 small secondary cover lines; leave breathing room so nothing crowds the face. CONSTRAINTS: keep the same face, hairstyle and identity unmistakably them — no beautifying into a stranger; all cover text must be short, correctly spelled and legible (no gibberish); one person; no real-world brand logos or barcodes; natural hands. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished magazine cover only, then on a new line one short sentence I can paste back to change the magazine theme or rewrite the cover line.

PromptHint: Attach your selfie, choose the [ADVENTURE SETTING] (e.g. snow summit, jungle ruins, ocean cliff) and an optional [GEAR] + [WEATHER].
Prompt: ROLE: You are an adventure-campaign photographer and compositor working inside a conversational image editor. TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and place the same person as the hero of an epic [ADVENTURE SETTING], looking like a real expedition shot. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide the camera height and scale that make the landscape feel vast, the natural light direction, the [GEAR] that fits the setting, and how [WEATHER] should affect light, hair and clothing. THEN EDIT: dress them in believable adventure gear, place them in the sweeping environment with correct scale and perspective, relight the subject to match the scene's sun/sky, and add atmosphere — wind in the hair, mist, spray, or blowing snow — so they're truly embedded in it. Title: Twin Selfie — Two Looks Of Me, Side By Side
Teaser: Splits one selfie into a single frame showing two contrasting versions of you side by side — e.g. corporate vs. street, or day vs. night — same face, two complete restyles.
PromptHint: Attach your selfie, then define [LOOK A] and [LOOK B] (the two styles), and an optional shared [BACKDROP STYLE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a portrait compositor and stylist building a split-frame inside a conversational image editor. TASK: Take the attached selfie [SELFIE] and produce ONE image showing the same person twice, side by side, styled as [LOOK A] on the left and [LOOK B] on the right. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): decide how to keep one consistent face across both panels, how the wardrobe, hair and lighting differ between the two looks, and how to keep a clean center seam so it reads as a deliberate diptych. THEN EDIT: render the left figure fully styled as [LOOK A] and the right figure fully styled as [LOOK B], each with its own fitting light, wardrobe and grade, against a coherent shared [BACKDROP STYLE]; keep both figures clearly the SAME person — identical face, just different styling. CONSTRAINTS: both faces must be the same recognizable person from the selfie — no drift into two different people, no beautifying; exactly two figures, one per side; natural hands; balanced composition; no text or logos. OUTPUT FORMAT: return the finished side-by-side image only, then on a new line one short sentence I can paste back to change either look or swap which side is which.
